name: Dimble
concept: A palm-sized, bottom-heavy deep-sea toy creature whose entire rounded belly is a frosted lamp-dome that dims to a shy ember when it's nervous and swells to a warm honey glow when it decides it likes you.
imagePrompt:
A single chubby deep-sea creature rendered as a premium moulded-vinyl designer toy, full body, standing, facing the viewer, symmetrical and centered.

SILHOUETTE: one clean, closed, unmistakable shape — a fat gumdrop / river-pebble body, widest at the bottom, tapering to a soft rounded crown. It sits on a broad flat rocker base like a weighted weeble, no legs. Two thick paddle flippers are fused directly to its sides, curving slightly forward like little mittens. A single chunky, rounded dome-crest sits on top of the head — a soft thumb-shaped fin lobe, no spikes, no strands. A short blunt tail-nub pokes from the lower back, just visible in profile. Every part is thick, fused and continuous: nothing detached, nothing thin, nothing wispy. Minimum limb thickness is generous and toy-safe.

PROPORTIONS: head-and-body are one unit, roughly 2 heads tall total. Eyes sit low and wide, in the bottom third of the face. Big cranium volume, tiny features, heavy base — the silhouette of something you want to catch when it wobbles.

THE ONE IDEA — THE BELLY LAMP: the entire lower front of the body is a frosted translucent dome, like the milky glass of a bedside nightlight, set flush into the vinyl with a visible moulded rim. Inside it, a warm honey-amber glow blooms outward, brightest at the core and softening to cream at the edges. The light spills gently onto the inner faces of the flippers and the underside of its chin, tinting them warm — it is lighting itself from within. The glow is contained entirely inside the body outline; no rays, no sparks, no floating motes, no light escaping into the air.

PALETTE: deep inky indigo-navy body with a dusty periwinkle speckle-spray across the crown and back; warm honey-amber and cream in the belly dome; coral-pink blush on the cheeks and on the undersides of the flippers; a thin band of teal shadow where the body meets its base.

MATERIALS — 3D TOY: matte-satin moulded vinyl with a gentle soft-touch sheen, a fine mould seam running visibly down each side of the body, slightly rounded-over edges, subtle dust-fine surface texture. The belly is frosted, sub-surface-scattering plastic. Soft studio product-photography lighting, big soft key from the upper left, gentle rim light along the crown, believable contact shadow under the flat base.

FACE: two large glossy dark-brown eyes, wide-set, each with one clean white highlight and a faint warm amber reflection from its own belly-lamp. No nose. A small, simple, closed smile — hopeful and a bit shy, the expression of a creature that just turned its light on for you and is waiting to see what you think. Soft coral cheek patches.

Clean, definite, readable outer silhouette. No text, no symbols, no fine detail smaller than a chunky brushstroke.
